# Transformation Understanding

_Last updated: 2026-04-19 | Session: 1554c0f6fdf9_

---

## Scenario

**One scenario confirmed by uploads:**
Transform a nested Orders JSON API payload into two Postgres staging tables.
No other scenario is implied by the uploaded evidence.

---

## Source Analysis

### Format & Access
| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Format | JSON (REST API response) |
| Evidence file | `uploads/orders_api_response.json` |
| Nesting depth | 3 levels: batch → order → items / customer / shippingAddress |
| Top-level fields | `batchId`, `generatedAt`, `orders[]` |

### Source Field Inventory

**Batch level (verified):**
| Field | Type | Nullable | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|-------|
| batchId | string | No | "ORD-2026-04-19-A" |
| generatedAt | ISO 8601 string | No | Batch timestamp — **NOT mapped to any target column** |
| orders | array | No | 3 records in sample |

**Order level (verified):**
| Field | Type | Nullable | Observed values |
|-------|------|----------|-----------------|
| orderId | string | No | A10001, A10002, A10003 |
| customer.id | string | No | C-001, C-002, C-003 |
| customer.name | string | No | Full name strings |
| customer.email | string | **Yes** | null on A10003 |
| shippingAddress.country | string | No | UK, India, Portugal |
| shippingAddress.city | string | No | Reading, Pune, Lisbon |
| items | array | No (but can be empty) | Empty on A10003 |
| status | string | No | shipped, processing, cancelled |
| createdAt | ISO 8601 string | No | 2026-04-18T… |
| discountCode | string | **Yes** | null (A10001, A10003), "APRIL10" (A10002) |

**Item level (verified):**
| Field | Type | Nullable | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|-------|
| sku | string | No | BK-100, PN-200, NB-300 |
| quantity | integer | No | |
| unitPrice | float | No | Stored as NUMERIC(12,2) in target |

### Source Quality Observations (Verified from Upload)
- **A10003**: `customer.email = null` — must be flagged in validation, not blocked
- **A10003**: `items = []` — empty array; must still produce a header row per mapping rules
- **`generatedAt`** is present at batch level but has no target column — unmapped field (see Gaps)
- `discountCode` is explicitly `null` (not absent) for non-discount orders
- 3 orders, 3 total items (2 on A10001, 1 on A10002, 0 on A10003)

---

## Target Contract Analysis

### Tables (Verified from DDL)

**`staging_order_header`**
| Column | Type | Constraints | Source |
|--------|------|-------------|--------|
| order_id | TEXT | PRIMARY KEY | order.orderId |
| customer_id | TEXT | NOT NULL | order.customer.id |
| customer_name | TEXT | nullable | order.customer.name |
| customer_email | TEXT | nullable | order.customer.email |
| shipping_country | TEXT | nullable | order.shippingAddress.country |
| shipping_city | TEXT | nullable | order.shippingAddress.city |
| order_status | TEXT | nullable | order.status |
| created_at | TIMESTAMP | nullable | order.createdAt (ISO→TIMESTAMP) |
| item_count | INTEGER | nullable | Derived: len(order.items) |
| order_total | NUMERIC(12,2) | nullable | Derived: Σ(quantity × unitPrice) |
| has_discount | BOOLEAN | nullable | Derived: discountCode IS NOT NULL |
| discount_code | TEXT | nullable | order.discountCode |
| source_batch_id | TEXT | nullable | top-level batchId |

**`staging_order_item`**
| Column | Type | Constraints | Source |
|--------|------|-------------|--------|
| order_id | TEXT | NOT NULL, PK part | order.orderId |
| line_number | INTEGER | NOT NULL, PK part | 1-based index in items[] |
| sku | TEXT | NOT NULL | item.sku |
| quantity | INTEGER | NOT NULL | item.quantity |
| unit_price | NUMERIC(12,2) | NOT NULL | item.unitPrice |
| line_total | NUMERIC(12,2) | NOT NULL | Derived: quantity × unit_price |

### Contract Gaps (Inferred / Not in DDL)
- **No FK constraint** declared between `staging_order_item.order_id` and `staging_order_header.order_id` — implicit ordering dependency exists (headers must insert before items)
- **No upsert semantics defined** in DDL — idempotency strategy is an open question (see Gaps)
- **No `updated_at` / `ingested_at` audit column** on either table
- **`order_status` is free TEXT** — no CHECK constraint or enum validation in DDL

---

## Mapping Rules (Verified)

### staging_order_header — One row per order

| Target Column | Source Path | Transformation |
|---------------|-------------|----------------|
| order_id | `order.orderId` | Direct copy |
| customer_id | `order.customer.id` | Direct copy |
| customer_name | `order.customer.name` | Direct copy |
| customer_email | `order.customer.email` | Direct copy; null allowed; flag in validation |
| shipping_country | `order.shippingAddress.country` | Direct copy |
| shipping_city | `order.shippingAddress.city` | Direct copy |
| order_status | `order.status` | Direct copy |
| created_at | `order.createdAt` | ISO 8601 → datetime (strip trailing Z) |
| item_count | `len(order.items)` | Derived; 0 when items=[] |
| order_total | `Σ(item.quantity × item.unitPrice)` | Decimal(0.00) when items=[] |
| has_discount | `order.discountCode is not None` | Boolean derivation |
| discount_code | `order.discountCode` | Direct copy; null allowed |
| source_batch_id | `payload.batchId` | Batch-level; same value on every row |

### staging_order_item — One row per item, no rows when items=[]

| Target Column | Source Path | Transformation |
|---------------|-------------|----------------|
| order_id | `order.orderId` | FK to header |
| line_number | `enumerate(order.items, start=1)` | 1-based; PK component |
| sku | `item.sku` | Direct copy |
| quantity | `item.quantity` | Cast to INTEGER |
| unit_price | `item.unitPrice` | Cast to NUMERIC(12,2) via Decimal |
| line_total | `item.quantity × item.unitPrice` | Rounded to 2dp via Decimal |

### Expected Row Counts for Sample Payload

| Order | Header rows | Item rows |
|-------|------------|-----------|
| A10001 | 1 | 2 (BK-100, PN-200) |
| A10002 | 1 | 1 (NB-300) |
| A10003 | 1 | 0 (empty items; cancelled) |
| **Total** | **3** | **3** |

### Expected Computed Values

| Order | item_count | order_total | has_discount |
|-------|-----------|-------------|--------------|
| A10001 | 2 | 29.00 | False |
| A10002 | 1 | 21.75 | True |
| A10003 | 0 | 0.00 | False |

---

## Gaps and Open Questions

| # | Gap | Impact | Status |
|---|-----|--------|--------|
| G1 | `generatedAt` (batch timestamp) has no target column | Low — data is silently dropped unless a column is added | **Open** |
| G2 | No upsert/idempotency strategy defined | Medium — reprocessing same batch may cause PK conflicts | **Open** (script uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING as interim) |
| G3 | No FK constraint in DDL for item→header | Low for staging; medium if downstream assumes referential integrity | **Noted** |
| G4 | `order_status` values not validated against an enum | Low — any string accepted; unexpected statuses pass silently | **Open** |
| G5 | No `ingested_at` / `updated_at` audit column | Low — no load timestamp available for operational debugging | **Open** |
| G6 | Currency not captured | Low — single-currency assumed; prices stored as NUMERIC(12,2) | **Assumed** |
| G7 | Float→Decimal precision risk on `unitPrice` | Low — mitigated by passing through str() before Decimal() | **Handled in code** |

---

## Inferred Assumptions

- All monetary values are in a single currency (no currency field in source or target)
- `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` is an acceptable interim idempotency strategy pending a confirmed upsert policy
- `created_at` timezone is UTC (Z suffix converted to +00:00)
- `line_number` ordering follows the array order in the source JSON
- `staging` prefix implies downstream tables exist; this is a landing zone, not a final model

---

## Missing Required Inputs

_None that would block the v1 implementation._ The gaps above (G1–G6) are clarification items for production hardening, not blockers for generating and running the transform.

# Data Dependency Map

_Last updated: 2026-04-19 | Session: 1554c0f6fdf9_

---

## Lineage Overview

```
orders_api_response.json
        │
        ├── payload.batchId  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │                                                                               │
        └── payload.orders[]                                                            │
                │                                                                       │
                ├── order.orderId ─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
                ├── order.customer.{id, name, email} ─────────────────────────┐    │   │
                ├── order.shippingAddress.{country, city} ────────────────┐   │    │   │
                ├── order.{status, createdAt, discountCode} ──────────┐   │   │    │   │
                │                                                      │   │   │    │   │
                │   DERIVED: len(items) → item_count                  │   │   │    │   │
                │   DERIVED: Σ(qty×price) → order_total               │   │   │    │   │
                │   DERIVED: discountCode IS NOT NULL → has_discount   │   │   │    │   │
                │                                                      ▼   ▼   ▼    ▼   ▼
                │                                              staging_order_header
                │
                └── order.items[]{sku, quantity, unitPrice}
                        │
                        │   DERIVED: enumerate(start=1) → line_number
                        │   DERIVED: qty × unit_price → line_total
                        │
                        ▼
                staging_order_item
```

---

## Node Inventory

| Node | Type | Role |
|------|------|------|
| `orders_api_response.json` | Source file | API payload, batch envelope |
| `transform_orders()` | Python function | Flattening, derivation, validation |
| `validate_result()` | Python function | Post-transform reconciliation |
| `staging_order_header` | Postgres table | One row per order |
| `staging_order_item` | Postgres table | One row per item |

---

## Field-Level Lineage

### → staging_order_header

| Target Field | Source Node | Source Field | Transform |
|--------------|-------------|--------------|-----------|
| order_id | orders[] | orderId | pass-through |
| customer_id | orders[].customer | id | pass-through |
| customer_name | orders[].customer | name | pass-through |
| customer_email | orders[].customer | email | pass-through (nullable) |
| shipping_country | orders[].shippingAddress | country | pass-through |
| shipping_city | orders[].shippingAddress | city | pass-through |
| order_status | orders[] | status | pass-through |
| created_at | orders[] | createdAt | ISO 8601 Z → datetime |
| item_count | orders[].items | len(items) | aggregate |
| order_total | orders[].items | Σ(quantity×unitPrice) | aggregate + round(2dp) |
| has_discount | orders[] | discountCode | IS NOT NULL → bool |
| discount_code | orders[] | discountCode | pass-through (nullable) |
| source_batch_id | payload | batchId | pass-through (batch-scoped) |

### → staging_order_item

| Target Field | Source Node | Source Field | Transform |
|--------------|-------------|--------------|-----------|
| order_id | orders[] | orderId | FK link to header |
| line_number | orders[].items | index | enumerate(start=1) |
| sku | orders[].items[] | sku | pass-through |
| quantity | orders[].items[] | quantity | cast to int |
| unit_price | orders[].items[] | unitPrice | Decimal, round(2dp) |
| line_total | orders[].items[] | quantity×unitPrice | multiply + round(2dp) |

---

## Execution Sequencing

```
Step 1: Parse JSON payload
Step 2: Extract batchId (used on every header row)
Step 3: FOR EACH order in orders[]:
    Step 3a: Detect duplicate orderId → skip + warn if found
    Step 3b: Flatten customer + shippingAddress fields
    Step 3c: FOR EACH item in items[] (1-based):
                 Compute line_total
                 Emit item row
    Step 3d: Aggregate item_count, order_total
    Step 3e: Derive has_discount
    Step 3f: Emit header row
Step 4: validate_result() reconciliation checks
Step 5: Load headers → staging_order_header  (must precede items)
Step 6: Load items   → staging_order_item
```

**Critical ordering constraint:** Headers MUST be inserted before items because of the implicit FK dependency (`staging_order_item.order_id` references `staging_order_header.order_id`). No FK is declared in the DDL, but violating this order risks referential inconsistency downstream.

---

## Dependency Risk Register

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|------|----------|------------|
| Empty items[] on cancelled orders | Low | Handled — header still emitted; no item rows |
| Null customer.email | Low | Warning logged; transform continues |
| Duplicate orderId in same batch | Medium | Duplicate detected, second occurrence skipped with warning |
| Float precision on unitPrice | Low | Routed through `Decimal(str(value))` before arithmetic |
| Re-run of same batch | Medium | `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` on both tables (interim policy) |
| `generatedAt` not mapped | Low | Silently dropped; revisit if audit trail needed |

---

## Unmapped Source Fields

| Field | Location | Reason not mapped |
|-------|----------|-------------------|
| generatedAt | payload root | No target column exists in DDL |

---

## Objective

Deliver a production-ready Python transformation script that:
1. Parses an Orders API JSON payload
2. Flattens nested structures into two Postgres staging tables
3. Computes all derived fields inline
4. Runs post-transform reconciliation before loading
5. Loads via psycopg2 `executemany` with idempotent conflict handling

---

## Implementation Status

| Slice | Deliverable | Status |
|-------|-------------|--------|
| S1 | Source analysis + mapping rules | ✅ Complete |
| S2 | Python transform script (`orders_transform.py`) | ✅ Generated |
| S3 | Inline validation + reconciliation checks | ✅ Included in script |
| S4 | psycopg2 INSERT helpers with ON CONFLICT | ✅ Included in script |
| S5 | Session artifacts updated | ✅ Complete |
| S6 | Upsert / idempotency policy confirmed | ⏳ Pending (G2) |
| S7 | `generatedAt` mapping decision | ⏳ Pending (G1) |
| S8 | `order_status` enum validation | ⏳ Pending (G4) |
| S9 | Integration test with live Postgres connection | ⏳ Pending environment |

---

## Generated Deliverable

**File:** `orders_transform.py`  
**Output location:** session outputs folder  
**Runtime:** Python 3.10+ (uses `datetime.fromisoformat` with Z handling, dataclasses, Decimal)  
**Dependencies:** `psycopg2` (standard), stdlib only for transform logic

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| Decimal arithmetic | `Decimal(str(value))` | Avoids float binary precision errors on currency |
| Idempotency | `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` | Safe interim strategy; no duplicate PK errors on replay |
| line_number | `enumerate(start=1)` | Preserves source array order; forms PK component |
| Error model | Warn + continue on null email / duplicate | Non-fatal; lets caller decide whether to abort load |
| Insert order | Headers before items | Respects implicit FK dependency |
| Timestamp | `Z` → `+00:00` before `fromisoformat` | Python 3.10 compatibility |

---

## Validation Checks Implemented

| Check | Type | Expected result on sample |
|-------|------|--------------------------|
| Header count = order count | Reconciliation | 3 == 3 ✅ |
| Item row count = Σ items | Reconciliation | 3 == 3 ✅ |
| Per-order item_count in header = item rows emitted | Cross-check | Pass for all 3 ✅ |
| Per-order order_total = Σ line_totals | Cross-check | A10001=29.00, A10002=21.75, A10003=0.00 ✅ |
| PK uniqueness (order_id, line_number) | Uniqueness | No duplicates in sample ✅ |
| All orders have a header row | Coverage | A10003 (cancelled, empty) included ✅ |
| Null customer email | Warning | A10003 flagged ✅ |

| ID | Item | Owner | Priority |
|----|------|-------|----------|
| G1 | Decide if `generatedAt` needs a target column | Schema owner | Low |
| G2 | Confirm upsert semantics (DO NOTHING vs DO UPDATE) | Product / Eng | Medium |

| G6 | Confirm single-currency assumption or add currency field | Product | Low |

---

## Next Steps (Optional)

1. Run `python orders_transform.py orders_api_response.json` against the sample to verify expected row output
2. Stand up a local Postgres instance and run `target_postgres_schema.sql` to create tables
3. Wire in a psycopg2 connection and call `load_to_postgres(conn, result)`
4. Extend `validate_result()` with a `order_status` allowlist check once the enum is confirmed

input.description: Python transformation script: flattens Orders API JSON → staging_order_header + staging_order_item with inline validation and psycopg2 load helpers
input.content:
"""
orders_transform.py
-------------------
Transforms an Orders API JSON payload into two lists of row-dicts ready for
psycopg2 executemany INSERT into Postgres staging tables:

    staging_order_header   — one row per order
    staging_order_item     — one row per line item (zero rows for empty-items orders)

Evidence base
    Source : orders_api_response.json
    Target : target_postgres_schema.sql
    Rules  : mapping_expectations.md

Design notes
    * Decimal arithmetic throughout to avoid float binary-precision errors on currency.
    * ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING used for idempotent re-runs (interim policy — see G2 in
      delivery-implementation-plan.md for production upsert decision).
    * Null customer email is a WARNING, not a fatal error; the transform continues and
      the caller decides whether to abort the load.
    * Headers are inserted before items to respect the implicit FK dependency.

Python 3.10+ required (datetime.fromisoformat handles offset strings directly).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import logging
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from decimal import ROUND_HALF_UP, Decimal
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Type alias
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
Row = dict[str, Any]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Result containers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class ValidationWarning:
    order_id: str
    field: str
    message: str


@dataclass
class TransformResult:
    header_rows: list[Row] = field(default_factory=list)
    item_rows: list[Row] = field(default_factory=list)
    warnings: list[ValidationWarning] = field(default_factory=list)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: safe Decimal rounding to 2 decimal places
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _round2(value: float | int | Decimal) -> Decimal:
    """Convert any numeric to a Decimal rounded to 2dp via string to avoid
    float-to-Decimal precision artefacts (e.g. 12.5 → '12.5' → Decimal('12.50'))."""
    return Decimal(str(value)).quantize(Decimal("0.01"), rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Core transform
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def transform_orders(payload: dict) -> TransformResult:
    """
    Parse and flatten the Orders API payload.

    Flattening strategy
    -------------------
    1.  Extract batch-level metadata (batchId) once — reused on every header row.
    2.  Iterate over orders[] — emit ONE header row per order regardless of whether
        items[] is empty (handles cancelled orders with no items per mapping rules).
    3.  For each order, iterate over items[] using enumerate(start=1) to assign a
        stable 1-based line_number that forms the composite PK with order_id.
    4.  Compute ALL derived fields inline before emitting rows:
            line_total  = quantity × unit_price         (per item)
            item_count  = len(items)                    (per order)
            order_total = Σ line_totals                 (per order; Decimal(0.00) when empty)
            has_discount = discountCode is not None     (per order)
    5.  Collect ValidationWarnings for null emails and duplicate order IDs without
        aborting — the caller decides whether to proceed with the load.

    Parameters
    ----------
    payload : dict
        Parsed JSON dict (top-level batch envelope).

    Returns
    -------
    TransformResult
        .header_rows  — list of dicts ready for staging_order_header INSERT
        .item_rows    — list of dicts ready for staging_order_item INSERT
        .warnings     — list of ValidationWarning (non-fatal data quality flags)
    """
    result = TransformResult()
    seen_order_ids: set[str] = set()

    # ── Batch-level metadata ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    batch_id: str = payload.get("batchId", "")
    # NOTE: payload.generatedAt is intentionally not mapped — no target column exists.
    #       Revisit if an audit column is added to the schema (Gap G1).

    orders: list[dict] = payload.get("orders", [])

    for order in orders:
        order_id: str = order.get("orderId", "")

        # ── Duplicate order_id guard ─────────────────────────────────────────
        if order_id in seen_order_ids:
            logger.warning("Duplicate orderId '%s' — second occurrence skipped.", order_id)
            result.warnings.append(
                ValidationWarning(order_id, "orderId", "Duplicate order_id — row skipped")
            )
            continue
        seen_order_ids.add(order_id)

        customer: dict = order.get("customer", {})
        shipping: dict = order.get("shippingAddress", {})
        items: list[dict] = order.get("items", [])
        discount_code: str | None = order.get("discountCode")

        # ── Null email warning (non-fatal) ───────────────────────────────────
        customer_email: str | None = customer.get("email")
        if customer_email is None:
            logger.warning("Order '%s': customer.email is null.", order_id)
            result.warnings.append(
                ValidationWarning(order_id, "customer.email", "customer email is null")
            )

        # ── Timestamp parsing ────────────────────────────────────────────────
        # ISO 8601 with trailing Z → Python datetime (UTC-aware).
        created_at_raw: str | None = order.get("createdAt")
        created_at: datetime | None = None
        if created_at_raw:
            created_at = datetime.fromisoformat(created_at_raw.replace("Z", "+00:00"))

        # ── Item rows — flattened with 1-based line_number ───────────────────
        # Computed first so we can sum line_totals for order_total below.
        item_rows_for_order: list[Row] = []
        for line_number, item in enumerate(items, start=1):
            qty: int = int(item.get("quantity", 0))
            unit_price: Decimal = _round2(item.get("unitPrice", 0))
            line_total: Decimal = _round2(qty * unit_price)

            item_rows_for_order.append(
                {
                    "order_id": order_id,
                    "line_number": line_number,   # 1-based; PK component
                    "sku": item.get("sku", ""),
                    "quantity": qty,
                    "unit_price": unit_price,
                    "line_total": line_total,
                }
            )

        # ── Order-level derived aggregates ───────────────────────────────────
        item_count: int = len(item_rows_for_order)
        order_total: Decimal = _round2(
            sum(r["line_total"] for r in item_rows_for_order)
        )                                        # Decimal("0.00") when items=[]
        has_discount: bool = discount_code is not None

        # ── Header row ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        result.header_rows.append(
            {
                "order_id": order_id,
                "customer_id": customer.get("id", ""),
                "customer_name": customer.get("name"),
                "customer_email": customer_email,
                "shipping_country": shipping.get("country"),
                "shipping_city": shipping.get("city"),
                "order_status": order.get("status"),
                "created_at": created_at,
                "item_count": item_count,
                "order_total": order_total,
                "has_discount": has_discount,
                "discount_code": discount_code,
                "source_batch_id": batch_id,
            }
        )

        # ── Item rows (empty list for orders with items=[], e.g. cancelled) ──
        result.item_rows.extend(item_rows_for_order)

    return result


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Post-transform validation / reconciliation
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def validate_result(payload: dict, result: TransformResult) -> list[str]:
    """
    Run reconciliation checks against the original payload.

    Returns a list of failure strings.  An empty list means all checks passed.
    Warnings collected during transform (e.g. null email) are separate from
    these hard reconciliation checks.

    Checks
    ------
    1. Header count == order count in payload
    2. Item row count == Σ len(items) across all orders
    3. Per-order: header.item_count == number of item rows emitted for that order
    4. Per-order: header.order_total == Σ line_totals for that order
    5. PK uniqueness on (order_id, line_number)
    6. Every order in payload has at least a header row (coverage check)
    """
    failures: list[str] = []
    orders = payload.get("orders", [])

    # 1. Header count
    expected_headers = len(orders)
    actual_headers = len(result.header_rows)
    if actual_headers != expected_headers:
        failures.append(
            f"Header count mismatch: expected {expected_headers}, got {actual_headers}"
        )

    # 2. Item row count
    expected_items = sum(len(o.get("items", [])) for o in orders)
    actual_items = len(result.item_rows)
    if actual_items != expected_items:
        failures.append(
            f"Item row count mismatch: expected {expected_items}, got {actual_items}"
        )

    # Index item rows by order_id for per-order checks
    item_rows_by_order: dict[str, list[Row]] = {}
    for r in result.item_rows:
        item_rows_by_order.setdefault(r["order_id"], []).append(r)

    for header in result.header_rows:
        oid = header["order_id"]
        actual_line_count = len(item_rows_by_order.get(oid, []))

        # 3. item_count cross-check
        if header["item_count"] != actual_line_count:
            failures.append(
                f"Order {oid}: header.item_count={header['item_count']} "
                f"but {actual_line_count} item rows emitted"
            )

        # 4. order_total cross-check
        computed_total = _round2(
            sum(r["line_total"] for r in item_rows_by_order.get(oid, []))
        )
        if header["order_total"] != computed_total:
            failures.append(
                f"Order {oid}: header.order_total={header['order_total']} "
                f"but sum of line_totals={computed_total}"
            )

    # 5. PK uniqueness on (order_id, line_number)
    seen_pks: set[tuple] = set()
    for r in result.item_rows:
        pk = (r["order_id"], r["line_number"])
        if pk in seen_pks:
            failures.append(f"Duplicate item PK: order_id={pk[0]}, line_number={pk[1]}")
        seen_pks.add(pk)

    # 6. Coverage — every order in payload must have a header row
    emitted_order_ids = {h["order_id"] for h in result.header_rows}
    for order in orders:
        oid = order.get("orderId")
        if oid and oid not in emitted_order_ids:
            failures.append(f"Order {oid} present in payload but missing from header rows")

    return failures


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Postgres INSERT helpers  (psycopg2 executemany-compatible)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

HEADER_INSERT_SQL = """
INSERT INTO staging_order_header (
    order_id, customer_id, customer_name, customer_email,
    shipping_country, shipping_city, order_status, created_at,
    item_count, order_total, has_discount, discount_code, source_batch_id
) VALUES (
    %(order_id)s, %(customer_id)s, %(customer_name)s, %(customer_email)s,
    %(shipping_country)s, %(shipping_city)s, %(order_status)s, %(created_at)s,
    %(item_count)s, %(order_total)s, %(has_discount)s, %(discount_code)s, %(source_batch_id)s
)
ON CONFLICT (order_id) DO NOTHING;
"""
# NOTE: Replace DO NOTHING with DO UPDATE SET … once the idempotency policy is confirmed (Gap G2).

ITEM_INSERT_SQL = """
INSERT INTO staging_order_item (
    order_id, line_number, sku, quantity, unit_price, line_total
) VALUES (
    %(order_id)s, %(line_number)s, %(sku)s, %(quantity)s, %(unit_price)s, %(line_total)s
)
ON CONFLICT (order_id, line_number) DO NOTHING;
"""


def load_to_postgres(conn, result: TransformResult) -> None:
    """
    Load transformed rows into Postgres via psycopg2.

    Ordering: headers are inserted before items to respect the implicit FK
    dependency (staging_order_item.order_id → staging_order_header.order_id).
    Both INSERTs are wrapped in a single transaction; conn.commit() is called
    once at the end.

    Parameters
    ----------
    conn : psycopg2 connection
    result : TransformResult returned by transform_orders()
    """
    with conn.cursor() as cur:
        if result.header_rows:
            cur.executemany(HEADER_INSERT_SQL, result.header_rows)
            logger.info("Inserted %d header rows.", len(result.header_rows))
        if result.item_rows:
            cur.executemany(ITEM_INSERT_SQL, result.item_rows)
            logger.info("Inserted %d item rows.", len(result.item_rows))
    conn.commit()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CLI entry point — useful for smoke-testing against the sample payload
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import sys

    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(levelname)s: %(message)s")

    payload_path = (
        Path(sys.argv[1]) if len(sys.argv) > 1 else Path("orders_api_response.json")
    )
    payload = json.loads(payload_path.read_text())

    result = transform_orders(payload)

    # ── Reconciliation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    failures = validate_result(payload, result)
    if failures:
        for f in failures:
            logger.error("VALIDATION FAILURE: %s", f)
        sys.exit(1)

    # ── Data-quality warnings ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    if result.warnings:
        for w in result.warnings:
            logger.warning("DATA QUALITY [%s / %s]: %s", w.order_id, w.field, w.message)

    # ── Summary ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    logger.info("All reconciliation checks passed.")
    logger.info("Header rows : %d", len(result.header_rows))
    logger.info("Item rows   : %d", len(result.item_rows))

    # ── Pretty-print for inspection ──────────────────────────────────────────
    print("\n=== staging_order_header ===")
    for row in result.header_rows:
        print(row)

    print("\n=== staging_order_item ===")
    for row in result.item_rows:
        print(row)

    # To load into Postgres, add:
    #   import psycopg2
    #   conn = psycopg2.connect("host=... dbname=... user=... password=...")
    #   load_to_postgres(conn, result)
    #   conn.close()

# Transformation Understanding

_Last updated: 2026-04-19 | Session: 1554c0f6fdf9_

---

## Scenario

**One scenario confirmed by uploads:**
Transform a nested Orders JSON API payload into two Postgres staging tables.
No other scenario is implied by the uploaded evidence.

---

## Source Analysis

### Format & Access
| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Format | JSON (REST API response) |
| Evidence file | `uploads/orders_api_response.json` |
| Nesting depth | 3 levels: batch → order → items / customer / shippingAddress |
| Top-level fields | `batchId`, `generatedAt`, `orders[]` |

### Source Field Inventory

**Batch level (verified):**
| Field | Type | Nullable | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|-------|
| batchId | string | No | "ORD-2026-04-19-A" |
| generatedAt | ISO 8601 string | No | Batch timestamp — **NOT mapped to any target column** |
| orders | array | No | 3 records in sample |

**Order level (verified):**
| Field | Type | Nullable | Observed values |
|-------|------|----------|-----------------|
| orderId | string | No | A10001, A10002, A10003 |
| customer.id | string | No | C-001, C-002, C-003 |
| customer.name | string | No | Full name strings |
| customer.email | string | **Yes** | null on A10003 |
| shippingAddress.country | string | No | UK, India, Portugal |
| shippingAddress.city | string | No | Reading, Pune, Lisbon |
| items | array | No (but can be empty) | Empty on A10003 |
| status | string | No | shipped, processing, cancelled |
| createdAt | ISO 8601 string | No | 2026-04-18T… |
| discountCode | string | **Yes** | null (A10001, A10003), "APRIL10" (A10002) |

**Item level (verified):**
| Field | Type | Nullable | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|-------|
| sku | string | No | BK-100, PN-200, NB-300 |
| quantity | integer | No | |
| unitPrice | float | No | Stored as NUMERIC(12,2) in target |

### Source Quality Observations (Verified from Upload)
- **A10003**: `customer.email = null` — must be flagged in validation, not blocked
- **A10003**: `items = []` — empty array; must still produce a header row per mapping rules
- **`generatedAt`** is present at batch level but has no target column — unmapped field (see Gaps)
- `discountCode` is explicitly `null` (not absent) for non-discount orders
- 3 orders, 3 total items (2 on A10001, 1 on A10002, 0 on A10003)

---

## Target Contract Analysis

### Tables (Verified from DDL)

**`staging_order_header`**
| Column | Type | Constraints | Source |
|--------|------|-------------|--------|
| order_id | TEXT | PRIMARY KEY | order.orderId |
| customer_id | TEXT | NOT NULL | order.customer.id |
| customer_name | TEXT | nullable | order.customer.name |
| customer_email | TEXT | nullable | order.customer.email |
| shipping_country | TEXT | nullable | order.shippingAddress.country |
| shipping_city | TEXT | nullable | order.shippingAddress.city |
| order_status | TEXT | nullable | order.status |
| created_at | TIMESTAMP | nullable | order.createdAt (ISO→TIMESTAMP) |
| item_count | INTEGER | nullable | Derived: len(order.items) |
| order_total | NUMERIC(12,2) | nullable | Derived: Σ(quantity × unitPrice) |
| has_discount | BOOLEAN | nullable | Derived: discountCode IS NOT NULL |
| discount_code | TEXT | nullable | order.discountCode |
| source_batch_id | TEXT | nullable | top-level batchId |

**`staging_order_item`**
| Column | Type | Constraints | Source |
|--------|------|-------------|--------|
| order_id | TEXT | NOT NULL, PK part | order.orderId |
| line_number | INTEGER | NOT NULL, PK part | 1-based index in items[] |
| sku | TEXT | NOT NULL | item.sku |
| quantity | INTEGER | NOT NULL | item.quantity |
| unit_price | NUMERIC(12,2) | NOT NULL | item.unitPrice |
| line_total | NUMERIC(12,2) | NOT NULL | Derived: quantity × unit_price |

### Contract Gaps (Inferred / Not in DDL)
- **No FK constraint** declared between `staging_order_item.order_id` and `staging_order_header.order_id` — implicit ordering dependency exists (headers must insert before items)
- **No upsert semantics defined** in DDL — idempotency strategy is an open question (see Gaps)
- **No `updated_at` / `ingested_at` audit column** on either table
- **`order_status` is free TEXT** — no CHECK constraint or enum validation in DDL

---

## Mapping Rules (Verified)

### staging_order_header — One row per order

| Target Column | Source Path | Transformation |
|---------------|-------------|----------------|
| order_id | `order.orderId` | Direct copy |
| customer_id | `order.customer.id` | Direct copy |
| customer_name | `order.customer.name` | Direct copy |
| customer_email | `order.customer.email` | Direct copy; null allowed; flag in validation |
| shipping_country | `order.shippingAddress.country` | Direct copy |
| shipping_city | `order.shippingAddress.city` | Direct copy |
| order_status | `order.status` | Direct copy |
| created_at | `order.createdAt` | ISO 8601 → datetime (strip trailing Z) |
| item_count | `len(order.items)` | Derived; 0 when items=[] |
| order_total | `Σ(item.quantity × item.unitPrice)` | Decimal(0.00) when items=[] |
| has_discount | `order.discountCode is not None` | Boolean derivation |
| discount_code | `order.discountCode` | Direct copy; null allowed |
| source_batch_id | `payload.batchId` | Batch-level; same value on every row |

### staging_order_item — One row per item, no rows when items=[]

| Target Column | Source Path | Transformation |
|---------------|-------------|----------------|
| order_id | `order.orderId` | FK to header |
| line_number | `enumerate(order.items, start=1)` | 1-based; PK component |
| sku | `item.sku` | Direct copy |
| quantity | `item.quantity` | Cast to INTEGER |
| unit_price | `item.unitPrice` | Cast to NUMERIC(12,2) via Decimal |
| line_total | `item.quantity × item.unitPrice` | Rounded to 2dp via Decimal |

### Expected Row Counts for Sample Payload

| Order | Header rows | Item rows |
|-------|------------|-----------|
| A10001 | 1 | 2 (BK-100, PN-200) |
| A10002 | 1 | 1 (NB-300) |
| A10003 | 1 | 0 (empty items; cancelled) |
| **Total** | **3** | **3** |

### Expected Computed Values

| Order | item_count | order_total | has_discount |
|-------|-----------|-------------|--------------|
| A10001 | 2 | 29.00 | False |
| A10002 | 1 | 21.75 | True |
| A10003 | 0 | 0.00 | False |

---

## Gaps and Open Questions

| # | Gap | Impact | Status |
|---|-----|--------|--------|
| G1 | `generatedAt` (batch timestamp) has no target column | Low — data is silently dropped unless a column is added | **Open** |
| G2 | No upsert/idempotency strategy defined | Medium — reprocessing same batch may cause PK conflicts | **Open** (script uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING as interim) |
| G3 | No FK constraint in DDL for item→header | Low for staging; medium if downstream assumes referential integrity | **Noted** |
| G4 | `order_status` values not validated against an enum | Low — any string accepted; unexpected statuses pass silently | **Open** |
| G5 | No `ingested_at` / `updated_at` audit column | Low — no load timestamp available for operational debugging | **Open** |
| G6 | Currency not captured | Low — single-currency assumed; prices stored as NUMERIC(12,2) | **Assumed** |
| G7 | Float→Decimal precision risk on `unitPrice` | Low — mitigated by passing through str() before Decimal() | **Handled in code** |

---

## Inferred Assumptions

- All monetary values are in a single currency (no currency field in source or target)
- `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` is an acceptable interim idempotency strategy pending a confirmed upsert policy
- `created_at` timezone is UTC (Z suffix converted to +00:00)
- `line_number` ordering follows the array order in the source JSON
- `staging` prefix implies downstream tables exist; this is a landing zone, not a final model

---

## Missing Required Inputs

_None that would block the v1 implementation._ The gaps above (G1–G6) are clarification items for production hardening, not blockers for generating and running the transform.
